research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
finding: GAO reports rural US residents face fewer providers, longer travel, transportation barriers, and less broadband.
what_happened: Used for rural high-income access pattern.
product_or_system_implication: See implications files for product mapping.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
finding: Delayed metastatic cancer diagnosis after repeated back-pain presentations
what_happened: Multiple contacts occurred across ED, GP, nurse, physiotherapy and out-of-hours care; diagnosis came after repeated presentations.
what_was_missed: Red flags and repeated symptom pattern were not connected early enough.
product_or_system_implication: Same-symptom clustering, unresolved-problem tracking, named follow-up owner
notes: Continuity and IT systems must surface repeated unresolved symptoms and cancer history.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
finding: Missed bowel lesion on scan with delayed diagnosis
what_happened: Abnormality was not identified and later follow-up was delayed.
what_was_missed: Scan finding missed and result follow-up failed.
product_or_system_implication: Closed-loop scan tracker with escalation for unreviewed abnormal results
notes: Imaging workflows need closed-loop review and patient-visible status.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
finding: Sepsis recognition failures repeated across NHS complaints
what_happened: Delayed diagnosis/treatment and communication/follow-up failures recurred across cases.
what_was_missed: Sepsis red flags and family concerns were not acted on quickly enough.
outcome_or_consequence: death or severe harm in complaint examples
product_or_system_implication: Family concern escalation field and sepsis reassessment prompts
notes: Emergency escalation must include family concern and deterioration tracking.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
finding: Bedouin community access and continuity barriers
what_happened: Physical access, health-literacy barriers, and out-of-system care complicate care continuity.
what_was_missed: Signals can be missed when care occurs outside the formal Israeli record system.
product_or_system_implication: Capture out-of-system visits and language/cultural access needs
notes: Integrated records alone are insufficient for marginalized periphery communities.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
finding: Non-allopathic provider TB referral intervention
what_happened: Intervention linked these providers to referral and free chest x-ray.
what_was_missed: TB identification improved; diagnostic delay reduced.
product_or_system_implication: Referral tools and diagnostic vouchers for informal/community providers
notes: Informal providers can be part of safer identification if connected to formal diagnostics.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
finding: Tanzania cancer diagnostic journey
what_happened: Diagnostic capacity such as imaging/pathology was concentrated in higher-level centers and patients often self-referred.
product_or_system_implication: Referral completion and diagnostic-capacity map
notes: Referral completion, funds, transport, and diagnostic capacity determine identification.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
condition_or_issue: malaria, pneumonia, diarrhea in children under 5
finding: WHO RAcE/iCCM CHW childhood illness workflow
what_happened: CHWs diagnosed, treated, and referred according to iCCM protocols.
what_was_missed: Danger signs and treatable childhood illnesses identified closer to community.
product_or_system_implication: Offline CHW protocols, stock status, and referral confirmation
notes: CHWs can be first-assessment nodes but require protocols, supplies, and referral loops.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
finding: Brian Sinclair emergency department waiting-room death
what_happened: He waited 34 hours in the ED; staff reportedly assumed intoxication.
what_was_missed: Clinical need and referral note were not acted on.
product_or_system_implication: Anti-bias triage audit, Indigenous liaison escalation, did-not-wait prevention
notes: Racism and social triage can block basic assessment and escalation.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
condition_or_issue: ED presentations by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander patients
finding: Queensland ED cultural-safety transformation project
what_happened: Project focused on culturally and clinically appropriate care and reducing did-not-wait outcomes.
what_was_missed: Risk of leaving before diagnostic workup was targeted.
product_or_system_implication: Real-time liaison routing and cultural-safety workflow
notes: Cultural safety must be operationalized in triage and ED flow.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
condition_or_issue: hepatitis C, diabetes risk, hypercholesterolemia, immunization gaps
finding: Health Catch-UP migrant screening
what_happened: EHR-integrated tool prompted screening.
what_was_missed: New conditions were identified and vaccination gaps surfaced.
outcome_or_consequence: 12 new conditions found among 99 screened migrants
product_or_system_implication: Migrant-specific EHR screening and vaccination reconstruction
notes: Targeted intake tools can identify hidden conditions when prior records are missing.
